item.title: Russia’s Oil Flows Slide as Drone, Storm Alerts Disrupt Key Port
item.description: Only one Russian crude cargo was loaded at Novorossiysk on the Black Sea last week

item.title: EDF Said to Plan Sale of Chinese Renewables Amid Nuclear Shift
item.description: Electricite de France SA is working with an adviser to sell its renewable energy assets in China as the French state-owned utility refocuses on European nuclear power projects, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: US Inflation Accelerates as Gas, Rent and Food Prices Climb
item.description: US inflation continued to accelerate in April amid an ongoing climb in gasoline prices driven by the Iran war and a jump in the cost of groceries.

item.title: SAP Invests in AI Automation Startup N8n at $5.2 Billion Value
item.description: SAP SE is investing in workflow automation platform n8n, doubling the startup’s valuation to $5.2 billion, as the German software giant builds out its artificial intelligence offering.

item.title: Brazil Inflation Picks Up Again, Nearing Top of Target Ceiling
item.description: Brazil’s inflation picked up as expected in April, pressured by more expensive food and fuel, to bump up against the top of the central bank’s target range.

item.title: Ramaphosa’s Political Peril Earns Shrug from Investors for Now
item.description: South Africa’s rand is shrugging off the threat that President Cyril Ramaphosa may be impeached, reflecting a bet that economic reforms he has championed will endure if he falls.

item.title: Waymo Recalls Robotaxis After Vehicle Drove on a Flooded Road
item.description: Waymo is recalling thousands of robotaxis to fix a software issue that could cause the autonomous vehicles to drive on flooded roads.

item.title: Stock Futures Drop on Hotter-Than-Forecast Core CPI, Oil Climbs
item.description: Traders pulled back from risk assets with a torrid run in US equities running out of steam as April inflation data came in hotter than economists’ estimates and worsening tensions between Washington and Iran sent oil prices higher.

item.title: Second Qatari LNG Tanker Exits Hormuz After Going Dark
item.description: A second Qatari liquefied natural gas tanker appeared to have exited the Strait of Hormuz, after a shipment successfully transited the chokepoint over the weekend.

item.title: Instructure Reaches Deal With Hackers to Return Canvas User Data
item.description: Instructure Inc. said it reached an agreement with hackers who disrupted universities’ online coursework and testing on the company’s Canvas platform, with the attackers returning stolen data and pledging not to extort the education software maker’s customers.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 12, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.4% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York amid a retreat in high-flying technology stocks.

item.title: AI Mania Makes Old-School Industrials Behave Like Chip Stocks
item.description: Optimism surrounding the potential for industrial companies to profit from the artificial intelligence boom has fueled record-setting momentum in the sector. Now worries are mounting that the group’s link to AI may be getting too tight.

item.title: The US and China Are Saving Oil From a Crisis
item.description: Record American exports and slowing Chinese imports are keeping a lid on prices — for now.

item.title: Analysis of US CPI Report for April
item.description: Pedestrians at a farmers market in Washington. Photographer: Al Drago/Bloomberg

item.title: Nvidia’s Huang Loses Out on Trump’s China Travel Plans
item.description: It’s not easy to remain in the president’s ever-rotating orbit

item.title: Intel’s $440 Billion Six-Week Surge Has Short Sellers Circling
item.description: A scorching rally in Intel Corp. shares is threatening huge losses for traders wagering that they’re due to fall. But that isn’t stopping them from placing those bets.

item.title: China, US Discuss Corn Purchases Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Chinese officials are in discussions with US counterparts to buy American crops, including corn, and could reach a deal during US President Donald Trump’s visit to China this week.

item.title: Wall Street Puts Blockchain to Work in $13 Trillion Repo Market
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. spent hundreds of millions of dollars over the course of more than a decade developing systems using blockchain, a novel technology that was supposed to radically upend financial markets, but has yet to become a game-changer.

item.title: EU Seeks to Overcome Complacency in Global Race for Rare Earths
item.description: With the US and China well ahead securing critical minerals, a fear of missing out consumes Brussels

item.title: Michigan Town Has a Potential US-China Investment Blueprint
item.description: Trump and Xi prepare to meet with investment deal seen as wild card

item.title: UK Approves Early Power-Project Funding in Race for Grid Parts
item.description: The UK approved more early construction funding for power-grid projects, as part of efforts to boost renewables generation and secure equipment amid supply-chain snarls and competition from other nations.

item.title: Swatch Quashes US Activist Attempt to Shake Up Hayek Stronghold
item.description: Shareholders of The Swatch Group AG rejected US activist investor Steven Wood’s push for a seat on the watchmaker’s board of directors for a second time, reinforcing the controlling Hayek family’s hold on the company.

item.title: India Inflation Inches Up as Iran War Drives Energy Costs
item.description: India’s inflation edged up last month as the war in the Middle East pushed up energy import costs.

item.title: Sea Tops Profit Estimates After Fending Off E-Commerce Rivals
item.description: Sea Ltd. reported quarterly earnings that topped analysts’ estimates after warding off rivals in Southeast Asia’s competitive e-commerce market rattled by economic and political challenges.

item.title: Australia Doomsday Scenario Sees Oil Hit $200 on War Escalation
item.description: Australia’s government conducted scenario analysis in its annual budget under which the war in Iran intensifies, driving oil to $200 a barrel and throwing the global economy into chaos.

item.title: Quiet Token Sales Boosted Trump Crypto Wealth by $660 Million
item.description: Trump family members made roughly $1.55 billion from sales of World Liberty Financial Inc. tokens, lifting their total fortune by about $660 million, after accounting for previously undisclosed transactions, according to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index.

item.title: EBay Rejects GameStop’s $56 Billion Takeover as Not Credible
item.description: EBay Inc. rejected a $56 billion takeover offer from GameStop Corp. Chief Executive Officer Ryan Cohen, calling the unsolicited bid “neither credible nor attractive” in a letter from Chairman Paul Pressler on Tuesday.

item.title: Bond Market Ditches Warsh Trade as Oil Surges
item.description: The bond market thought it had the Kevin Warsh trade figured out. Events changed that.

item.title: Business Confidence Sinks in Peru as Radical Candidate Nears Runoff
item.description: Peru’s chaotic presidential election is taking a toll on business sentiment as a radical candidate edges closer to a runoff following a prolonged vote count.

item.title: JD Profit Beats Estimates After China Curbs Food Delivery War
item.description: JD.com Inc. reported better-than-expected profit after Beijing clamped down on its fierce food delivery battle with Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and Meituan.
